description: Move rate limiting from nginx to application layer misses some entry points
diff_summary: Moved rate limiting from nginx to Python application middleware, applied manually per-route

edge_cases: Login endpoint has no rate limiting — brute force attacks now possible; health check, metrics, and any internal endpoints also unprotected
ground_truth.broken_pattern: Nginx rate limiting applied universally to all routes; application-level requires opt-in per route — any new route added is unprotected by default; request.remote_addr can be spoofed via X-Forwarded-For
ground_truth.next_prompt: We need to add rate limiting to the login endpoint — brute force protection is missing
ground_truth.notes: Moving from infrastructure to application-level security requires opt-in which creates gaps
